title: Viburnum sulcatum
intro: Viburnum sulcatum, also known as the mountain sweet pepperbush, is a deciduous shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Viburnum sulcatum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Viburnum sulcatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae. It is native to China, where it grows in the mountainous regions. This plant is a member of the genus Viburnum, which comprises over 150 species of deciduous shrubs. The classification of Viburnum sulcatum at the rank of genus and family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Further classification at the ranks of order, class, and kingdom positions it within the larger plant kingdom.
